title: leichhardtia glandulifera
intro: Leichhardtia glandulifera, also known as the "Tree Croton," is a species of flowering plant in the spurge family, Euphorbiaceae, native to Australia. The medicinal properties of Leichhardtia glandulifera lie in its sap, which has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including wounds, skin conditions, and fever. The health benefits of the plant are attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infection. Leichhardtia glandulifera can be prepared into herbal remedies such as tinctures, ointments, and infusions, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
